channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Anne Hathaway por fin está lista para la diversión
item.description: Conocida sobre todo por sus personajes tensos en grandes éxitos del cine, la actriz es una estrella del pop torturada en ‘Mother Mary’ y vuelve a su lado juguetón en ‘El diablo viste a la moda 2’.
item.dc:creator: Joe Coscarelli, Jon Caramanica and Thea Traff

media:content.media:credit: Thea Traff for The New York Times

item.title: Want to Be More Cultured? T Magazine Guides the Way.
item.description: A new issue aims to teach readers about essential films, wines and the best way to slurp an oyster, among other topics. Let the debates begin.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Ewing

item.title: Spelling Bee Hints for April 26, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Trump Safe After Shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: President Trump was at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner at a Washington hotel when a gunman opened fire. He was unharmed.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times

item.title: Cómo lidiar con personas difíciles
item.description: Estos seis libros pueden ayudar a aliviar tensiones.
item.dc:creator: Louisa Kamps

item.title: Cómo evitar las nuevas tarifas de equipaje en aerolíneas
item.description: Los pasajeros enfrentan tarifas más altas mientras la guerra con Irán eleva el precio del combustible de aviación. Los programas de viajero frecuente y ciertas tarjetas de crédito pueden ayudar a evitar estos cargos.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Sims

media:content.media:credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Con la subida de los precios del combustible de los aviones, las compañías aéreas aumentan las tarifas de equipaje para hacer frente a los costos adicionales. La mayoría de las principales compañías aéreas estadounidenses han aumentado la tarifa en unos 10 dólares.

item.title: Trump Describes His Mind-Set After Shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: Very little was clear about what had happened at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night. But the president wanted to talk about it.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump speaking in the White House briefing room on Saturday night.

item.title: Can an A.I. Company Ever Be Good?
item.description: The short answer: not really. The long answer: maybe with a little help.
item.dc:creator: Paul Ford

item.title: A Gunman Got Close to Trump Again, Raising More Questions About Political Violence
item.description: After two previous assassination attempts, the effort by an armed man to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner is sure to raise questions about security and political violence.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Heavy security was evident at the White House on Saturday night.

item.title: Confusion and Fright at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner After Shots Were Fired
item.description: A sense of danger spread like a wave among high-profile politicians and journalists as an emergency unfolded at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Secret Service members responding after the sound of gunfire at the Washington Hilton on Saturday.

item.title: California Man in Custody After Shooting at Dinner Attended by Trump
item.description: Shots were fired at the hotel hosting the White House correspondents’ dinner. Authorities said the attack was carried out by a lone gunman who was brought down by the Secret Service.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Emergency personnel raced to the Washington Hilton.

item.title: In Japan, the Fight Is on Against Unruly Tourists
item.description: Huge crowds of visitors during cherry blossom season have put a picturesque Japanese town on edge. Some residents are trying to take charge.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández, Kiuko Notoya and Kentaro Takahashi

media:content.media:description: Tourists in April in Fujiyoshida, Japan.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Has Nintendo Solved Its Princess Problem?
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, April 26, 2026.

item.title: What to Know About King Charles and Queen Camilla’s U.S. State Visit Hosted by Trump
item.description: The British royals are set to arrive on Monday for a visit hosted by President Trump, with a garden party, an address to Congress and a banquet on the schedule.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Ian Vogler
media:content.media:description: King Charles and Queen Camilla in London this past week. When President Trump was asked whether Charles’s upcoming visit could mend relations with Britain, he said: “Absolutely. He’s fantastic. He’s a fantastic man. Absolutely the answer is yes.”

item.title: 40 Years After the Meltdown, War Layers Another Disaster on Chernobyl
item.description: Ideas have been floated for how the contaminated zone could bring economic benefits to Ukraine. But for the foreseeable future, it will be an army-controlled security belt.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer, Evelina Riabenko and Brendan Hoffman

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times

item.title: 40 Years Ago, a Nuclear Catastrophe at Chernobyl
item.description: Photographs from the first days of the Chernobyl disaster and of the aftermath years later show the response, the evacuation and the long-term consequences of the world’s worst nuclear accident.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Gamma-Rapho, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Chernobyl nuclear power plant three days after the explosion in April 1986.

item.title: Zara Larsson’s Pop Career Had Stalled. But a Big Second Act Awaited.
item.description: The Swedish singer spent a decade toiling outside the spotlight. But a PinkPantheress remix, a rainbow-streaked dolphin and an ability to roll with the punches brought her back.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Horn and Caroline Tompkins

media:content.media:credit: Caroline Tompkins for The New York Times

item.title: How Nieves González’s Painting of Lily Allen Made Her Famous
item.description: Nieves González, a 29-year-old painter, once worked in relative obscurity in Andalusia. Her picture of the British singer Lily Allen changed that.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz

media:content.media:credit: Finbarr O'Reilly for The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: April 26, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, April 26, 2026.

item.title: Gunman Was Tackled by Law Enforcement Near Correspondents’ Dinner Security Checkpoint
item.description: The gunman did not make it inside the hotel ballroom where President Trump, top officials and hundreds of journalists had gathered for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Security at the Washington Hilton on Saturday.

item.title: Watch Live: Trump Speaks To Press After Reports of Shots Fired at Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: President Trump gives a news conference after he was rushed from the stage after gunfire broke out in the hotel where the White House correspondents’ dinner was being held on Saturday night

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times

item.title: Review: ‘Joe Turner’s Come and Gone,’ With Taraji P. Henson and Cedric the Entertainer
item.description: This revival starring Cedric the Entertainer and Taraji P. Henson may be uneven at times, but it still unlocks Wilson’s mysterious drama.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cedric the Entertainer, left, and Taraji P. Henson in “Joe Turner’s Come and Gone” at the Ethel Barrymore Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Live Updates: Trump Describes Gunman’s Attack at Washington Hotel
item.description: President Trump was rushed from the stage of a dinner hosted by the White House reporters’ association. Trump said a Secret Service agent was shot at but “saved” by his bulletproof vest. The F.B.I. said a suspect was in custody.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Fires Board Members of Group That Oversees U.S. Science Funding
item.description: The dismissals from an independent board that oversees the National Science Foundation marked the president’s latest assault on scientific research organizations.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:credit: Alex Wong/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The National Science Foundation in Arlington, Va.

item.title: How the War in Iran Is Draining the U.S. of Critical Weapons
item.description: The United States has blown through weapons as the cost of the war in Iran has hit nearly $1 billion a day. Our national security correspondent Eric Schmitt explains how American costs may go beyond the financial.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Gilad Thaler, Nour Idriss, Stephanie Swart, June Kim, Paul Abowd and Zach Wood
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Al Qaeda-Linked Militants Launch Major Attacks on Cities Across Mali
item.description: The armed group JNIM claimed to have seized two key cities and destroyed the defense minister’s residence in a coordinated offensive that experts said was a major escalation in yearslong hostilities.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: A Malian soldier on guard outside Bamako, Mali, on Saturday.

item.title: Well-Suited
item.description: Everything fits in Rebecca Goldstein’s beautifully crafted puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Matt Cardy/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A group of applicants for a resident witch position at Wookey Hole Caves in England; cackling and demonstrable hexing skills required.

item.title: Crushed at the Polls, Hungary’s Former Ruling Party Licks Its Wounds
item.description: Outgoing prime minister, Viktor Orban, who helped found the Fidesz party, said he would leave his Parliament seat but hoped to remain as head of his party.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins

media:content.media:credit: Bernadett Szabo/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Hungary’s outgoing prime minister, Viktor Orban, in Budapest earlier this month.

item.title: Eugene Braunwald, Whose Research Reshaped Cardiology, Dies at 96
item.description: His work changed how doctors understood heart attacks, heart failure and coronary artery disease, and helped lead to therapies that saved millions of lives.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Kandell

item.title: Families of Iranian Children Killed in School Airstrike Pen Letter to Pope
item.description: Pope Leo said he has seen the letter from the parents of more than 100 children killed in the strike, which a preliminary inquiry found resulted from a mistake by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial in Tehran for the victims of an airstrike on an elementary school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of U.S. and Israeli bombing of the country.

item.title: 2 C.I.A. Officers Killed in Mexico Crash Lacked Proper Authorization
item.description: The two Americans were killed on Sunday when their vehicle crashed while returning from an antidrug operation led by Mexico’s armed forces in the state of Chihuahua.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Attorney General'S Office of Chihuahua, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A suspected methamphetamine processing lab in the northern Mexican state of Chihuahua.

item.title: Big-Game Hunter From California Is Killed by Elephant in Gabon
item.description: Ernie Dosio, 75, of Lodi, Calif., was hunting a species of antelope in the dense forests of Gabon when his hunting party stumbled on a small herd of female elephants with calves.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

item.title: ‘Michael’ is Expected to Collect $200 Million in First Weekend, Shaking Off Bad Reviews
item.description: Lionsgate estimated on Saturday that the Michael Jackson biopic would collect more than $200 million over its first few days in theaters.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

media:content.media:credit: Lionsgate
media:content.media:description: Weekend ticket sales in North America for “Michael” could reach $95 million. Box office analysts had expected about $70 million.

item.title: Texas Can Arrest and Deport People Who Illegally Cross at Mexico Border, Court Says
item.description: The court that paused a 2023 law allowing state and local police officers to arrest migrants has now ruled that the measure is legal, a decision likely to be appealed.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin

media:content.media:credit: Cheney Orr for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Texas authorities now have the ability to arrest migrants who cross into the United States illegally. Many of those crossings have occurred along the Rio Grande.

item.title: In Deep Blue California, a G.O.P.-Backed Voter ID Proposal Makes the Ballot
item.description: A Republican-backed initiative has cleared the signature threshold for the November election. Critics say the measure could make it harder for people to vote.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Zaydee Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A voter casting his ballot in Fresno, Calif., last year.

item.title: A Year Later, Trump’s ‘Most Exclusive’ Memecoin Event Is a Lot Less Exclusive
item.description: Even a Times reporter qualified for the event, which caused outrage last year for providing access to President Trump in exchange for investment in one of his family’s crypto ventures.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany

media:content.media:credit: Tim Enthoven

item.title: Chicago Hospital Shooting Leaves 1 Officer Dead and Another in Critical Condition
item.description: A 57-year-old officer was “fighting for his life,” after the shooting on Saturday at Endeavor Health Swedish Hospital, the police superintendent said. A suspect was in custody.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: Anthony Vazquez/Chicago Sun-Times, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Two police officers were shot at Endeavor Health Swedish Hospital in Chicago on Saturday, the authorities said.

item.title: 18-Year-Old Charged in Fatal Shooting of Teenager in Queens Park
item.description: Zahir Davis was arrested on Friday night in connection with the killing of Jaden Pierre, 15, who was shot in broad daylight during a fight in South Jamaica.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The New York Police Department has struggled to address a recent rise in shootings involving teenagers, even as gun violence and other major crimes in the city have declined.

item.title: Semyon Gluzman, 79, Dies; Doctor Dared to Criticize Soviet Psychiatry
item.description: He was imprisoned for calling attention to the practice of declaring dissidents mentally ill. He later led efforts to confront his field’s dark past.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Sonia Pressman Fuentes, Early Women’s Rights Lawyer, Dies at 97
item.description: In a conversation with the feminist writer Betty Friedan, she planted the seed that became the National Organization for Women.
item.dc:creator: Trip Gabriel

media:content.media:credit: Virginia Mayo/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Sonia Pressman Fuentes at the Red Star Line Museum in Antwerp, Belgium, in 2013. Ms. Fuentes’ family was Jewish, and fled to the United States on the Red Star Line in 1934.

item.title: Trump Cancels Witkoff and Kushner’s Trip for Iran Talks, Saying, ‘We Have All the Cards.’
item.description: The last-minute scuttling of the trip to Pakistan was the latest sign of how far apart the two sides are on reaching a deal to end the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Thursday. He decided on Saturday not to have two of his top negotiators travel to Pakistan.

item.title: Every Black Republican Is Leaving the House, Erasing Diversity Gains
item.description: All four Black House Republicans are retiring after this year, a reflection of the striking and persistent lack of diversity in the G.O.P. ranks of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni

media:content.media:credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Rep. John James, R-Mich., left, watches as U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, right, speaks during a tour of Atomic Industries’ manufacturing facility.

item.title: La guerra y las sanciones aceleran el impulso monetario chino
item.description: El intento de China de construir un sistema financiero basado en el renminbi, fuera del alcance del dólar estadounidense, está ganando adeptos como forma de eludir las sanciones.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Lincoln Agnew

item.title: They Survived Torture. Why Is the Program That Helped Them Closing?
item.description: Some Bellevue Hospital officials feared having to disclose patients’ immigration status. They will get care elsewhere at the hospital, administrators say.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg
item.category: New York City Health and Hospitals Corp

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dr. Allen Keller, left, founded the torture survivors’ program, and Dr. Hawthorne Smith, the clinical director, started there 31 years ago as an intern.

item.title: At White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump Will Dine With Reporters He’s Been Roasting
item.description: President Trump has boycotted the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in previous years.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump spoke to the press before he departed the White House en route to Nevada and Arizona last week.
